findings.title: Disabled button uses 0.4 opacity — fails non-text contrast
findings.message: Reverting to `opacity: 0.4` on the disabled state pushes the button below the 3:1 non-text contrast threshold. Per the team's WCAG policy this is currently in remediation, so the bot flags it as non-blocking — but this change reintroduces an issue that was already addressed on master. Recommend keeping the slate-grey background pattern instead, or pairing the opacity with a parallel a11y-only signal (e.g. `aria-disabled` plus a tooltip explaining why it's disabled).
